---
editor_options:
  chunk_output_type: console
---

# When people come to Hubbard Hall

When the *same* production plays Friday, Saturday, **and** Sunday, which day do people pick?
This chapter leads with that within-run view — the cleanest read on audience day preference,
since it holds the show constant — then asks how the answer has changed over time, whether opera
and music runs behave the same way, and closes with the coarser all-events view. How runs are
reconstructed from Neon events, what is excluded, and how headcount is counted are in the
[Methodology](99-methodology.html) appendix.

## Within a theater run: Friday, Saturday, or Sunday?

Only theater production runs that played **all three** of Friday, Saturday, and Sunday — mostly
mainstage shows running one to four weekends. Attendance is true **headcount**, not
registrations.

```{python}
#| label: setup-runs
import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px
from hh.io import read_parquet
from hh.viz import style, BLUE, CATEGORICAL
from hh.analytics.timing import WEEKDAY_ORDER
from hh.analytics.productions import production_performances, full_weekend_runs, time_slot

regs = read_parquet("processed", "registrations_enriched.parquet")
perf = production_performances(regs, minorcats=("theater",))
fw = full_weekend_runs(perf)

day_order = [d for d in WEEKDAY_ORDER if d in set(fw["weekday"])]
day = (
    fw.groupby("weekday")
    .agg(performances=("swept_event_id", "count"), attendees=("attendees", "sum"))
    .reindex(day_order)
)
day["attendees per performance"] = day["attendees"] / day["performances"]
day["share of attendees"] = day["attendees"] / day["attendees"].sum()

n_runs = fw["production_run"].nunique()
yr_span = f"{fw['starts_on'].min():%Y}–{fw['starts_on'].max():%Y}"
sat_share = f"{day.loc['Saturday', 'share of attendees']:.0%}"
per_perf = {
    d: f"{day.loc[d, 'attendees per performance']:.0f}"
    for d in ("Friday", "Saturday", "Sunday")
}
```

Across the **`{python} f"{n_runs}"` qualifying production runs** (`{python} yr_span`),
**`{python} f"{len(fw):,}"` performances** drew **`{python} f"{int(fw['attendees'].sum()):,}"`
attendees**. Any weekday rows below Friday–Sunday are the rare midweek performances inside a
qualifying run (Winter Carnival 2015 played two Thursdays):

```{python}
#| label: tab-run-day
day.style.format(
    {
        "performances": "{:,}",
        "attendees": "{:,}",
        "attendees per performance": "{:.1f}",
        "share of attendees": "{:.1%}",
    }
)
```

**Saturday brings in the most people (`{python} sat_share` of attendees) — but only because most
runs play Saturday twice (matinee + evening). Per performance the three days are essentially
tied: Friday `{python} per_perf['Friday']`, Saturday `{python} per_perf['Saturday']`, and Sunday
`{python} per_perf['Sunday']` attendees per show.**

How each production splits across the three days (runs with fewer than 30 Fri–Sun attendees are
left off the chart but appear in the table below):

```{python}
#| label: fig-run-day-share
#| fig-cap: "Share of each run's Fri–Sun attendees by day — one bar per production run, oldest at top"
wk = fw[fw["weekday"].isin(["Friday", "Saturday", "Sunday"])]
run_day = wk.groupby(["production_run", "weekday"], as_index=False)["attendees"].sum()
run_day["share"] = run_day["attendees"] / run_day.groupby("production_run")[
    "attendees"
].transform("sum")
order = fw.groupby("production_run")["starts_on"].min().sort_values().index.tolist()
run_totals = wk.groupby("production_run")["attendees"].sum()
charted = [r for r in order if run_totals[r] >= 30]
DAY_COLORS = {"Friday": CATEGORICAL[0], "Saturday": CATEGORICAL[1], "Sunday": CATEGORICAL[2]}

fig = px.bar(
    run_day[run_day["production_run"].isin(charted)],
    x="share",
    y="production_run",
    color="weekday",
    orientation="h",
    category_orders={
        "production_run": charted[::-1],
        "weekday": ["Friday", "Saturday", "Sunday"],
    },
    color_discrete_map=DAY_COLORS,
    custom_data=["attendees"],
)
fig.update_traces(
    hovertemplate="%{x:.0%} — %{customdata[0]:,} attendees<extra>%{fullData.name}</extra>"
)
fig.update_layout(
    height=740,
    xaxis_tickformat=".0%",
    xaxis_title="Share of run's Fri–Sun attendees",
    yaxis_title="",
)
style(fig).show()
```

The same runs in detail — attendees by day, with each run's dates and performance count:

```{python}
#| label: tab-run-detail
by_day = (
    fw.pivot_table(
        index="production_run",
        columns="weekday",
        values="attendees",
        aggfunc="sum",
        fill_value=0,
    )
    .reindex(columns=day_order)
)
by_day["Total"] = by_day.sum(axis=1)
meta = fw.groupby("production_run").agg(
    first=("starts_on", "min"), last=("starts_on", "max"), performances=("swept_event_id", "count")
)
detail = meta.join(by_day).reindex(order)
detail["first"] = detail["first"].dt.strftime("%b %d, %Y")
detail["last"] = detail["last"].dt.strftime("%b %d, %Y")
detail.index.name = None
detail = detail.rename(columns={"first": "First", "last": "Last", "performances": "Perfs"})
detail.style.format("{:,}", subset=[c for c in detail.columns if c not in ("First", "Last")])
```

Saturday's totals hide one more split: most runs play a 2 pm matinee *and* an evening show that
day. Splitting Saturday performances by time slot:

```{python}
#| label: tab-sat-slot
sat = fw[fw["weekday"] == "Saturday"].assign(slot=lambda d: d["event_name"].map(time_slot))
slot = (
    sat.groupby("slot")
    .agg(performances=("swept_event_id", "count"), attendees=("attendees", "sum"))
    .reindex(["matinee", "evening"])
)
slot["attendees per performance"] = slot["attendees"] / slot["performances"]
mat_rate = f"{slot.loc['matinee', 'attendees per performance']:.0f}"
eve_rate = f"{slot.loc['evening', 'attendees per performance']:.0f}"
slot.style.format(
    {"performances": "{:,}", "attendees": "{:,}", "attendees per performance": "{:.1f}"}
)
```

**Saturday evenings draw `{python} eve_rate` attendees per show versus `{python} mat_rate` for
Saturday matinees — the weakest slot of the weekend. Sunday's single 2 pm show
(`{python} per_perf['Sunday']` per show) draws as well as any slot, so the matinee habit is a
Sunday habit, not a Saturday one.**

## Has the best day changed over time?

The numbers above pool 2013–2026, but the day ranking has not been stable. Still holding the
product constant (full-weekend theater runs, attendees per performance so days with two shows
don't get double credit), here is the same question across roughly five-year periods.

```{python}
#| label: setup-run-trends
PERIODS = ["2013–2015", "2016–2020", "2021–2026"]

def period(year):
    return pd.cut(year, bins=[2010, 2015, 2020, 2026], labels=PERIODS)

run_rate = (
    wk.groupby(["production_run", "weekday"], as_index=False)
    .agg(performances=("attendees", "count"), attendees=("attendees", "sum"))
)
run_rate = run_rate[run_rate["production_run"].isin(charted)].copy()
run_rate["rate"] = run_rate["attendees"] / run_rate["performances"]
run_rate["run_start"] = run_rate["production_run"].map(
    wk.groupby("production_run")["starts_on"].min()
)

recent = run_rate[run_rate["run_start"] >= "2021-01-01"]
best_day = recent.loc[recent.groupby("production_run")["rate"].idxmax(), "weekday"]
n_recent = recent["production_run"].nunique()
n_recent_sun = int((best_day == "Sunday").sum())

wk_periods = wk.assign(period=period(wk["starts_on"].dt.year))
pd_rate = (
    wk_periods.groupby(["period", "weekday"], observed=True)
    .agg(performances=("attendees", "count"), attendees=("attendees", "sum"))
    .reset_index()
)
pd_rate["rate"] = pd_rate["attendees"] / pd_rate["performances"]
rate_of = lambda p, d: pd_rate.set_index(["period", "weekday"])["rate"].loc[(p, d)]
```

One dot per run and day — each run's attendees per performance on Fridays, Saturdays, and
Sundays:

```{python}
#| label: fig-run-rate-over-time
#| fig-cap: "Attendees per performance by day, one point per full-weekend theater run"
fig = px.scatter(
    run_rate.sort_values("run_start"),
    x="run_start",
    y="rate",
    color="weekday",
    color_discrete_map=DAY_COLORS,
    category_orders={"weekday": ["Friday", "Saturday", "Sunday"]},
    hover_name="production_run",
    custom_data=["performances", "attendees"],
)
fig.update_traces(
    hovertemplate=(
        "<b>%{hovertext}</b><br>%{y:.0f} attendees/perf "
        "(%{customdata[1]:,} over %{customdata[0]} shows)<extra>%{fullData.name}</extra>"
    )
)
for d in ("Friday", "Saturday", "Sunday"):  # 5-run rolling average as a guide line
    s = run_rate[run_rate["weekday"] == d].sort_values("run_start")
    fig.add_scatter(
        x=s["run_start"],
        y=s["rate"].rolling(5, center=True, min_periods=3).mean(),
        mode="lines",
        line=dict(color=DAY_COLORS[d], width=1.5),
        opacity=0.6,
        showlegend=False,
        hoverinfo="skip",
    )
fig.update_layout(
    xaxis_title="Run opening date", yaxis_title="Attendees per performance", legend_title=""
)
style(fig).show()
```

Aggregated to periods:

```{python}
#| label: fig-run-rate-periods
#| fig-cap: "Attendees per performance by day and period, full-weekend theater runs only"
fig = px.bar(
    pd_rate,
    x="period",
    y="rate",
    color="weekday",
    barmode="group",
    color_discrete_map=DAY_COLORS,
    category_orders={"period": PERIODS, "weekday": ["Friday", "Saturday", "Sunday"]},
    custom_data=["performances"],
)
fig.update_traces(
    hovertemplate="%{y:.1f} attendees/perf (%{customdata[0]} shows)<extra>%{fullData.name}</extra>"
)
fig.update_layout(
    xaxis_title="Period", yaxis_title="Attendees per performance", legend_title=""
)
style(fig).show()
```

**Per-performance attendance has roughly doubled across the periods, and the day ranking has
flipped. In 2013–2015 Friday was the strongest day
(`{python} f"{rate_of('2013–2015', 'Friday'):.0f}"` per show vs
`{python} f"{rate_of('2013–2015', 'Sunday'):.0f}"` on Sunday); in 2016–2020 the days were about
even; since 2021 Sunday is clearly strongest —
`{python} f"{rate_of('2021–2026', 'Sunday'):.0f}"` per show vs about
`{python} f"{rate_of('2021–2026', 'Friday'):.0f}"` on Friday and Saturday. Sunday has been the
best-attended day in `{python} f"{n_recent_sun}"` of the `{python} f"{n_recent}"` full-weekend
runs since 2021.**

Recent-period caveats: 2021–2026 rests on `{python} f"{n_recent}"` runs
(`{python} f"{int(wk_periods[wk_periods['period'] == '2021–2026'].shape[0])}"` performances),
and recent runs rarely schedule Saturday matinees, which used to pull Saturday's average down —
yet Sunday leads anyway.

## Do opera and music runs behave the same way?

Music and opera get the same treatment: map event names to productions, keep runs that played
all of Friday, Saturday, and Sunday. Almost all music events are one-off concerts (Music from
Salem plays single afternoons, cabarets play one or two nights), so nearly everything that
qualifies is opera — the summer festival stagings of 2013–2017, which also played real midweek
(Wednesday/Thursday) performances — plus one 2015 musical folktale with a theater-style
four-weekend run. No over-time comparison is possible: the qualifying runs all fall in
2013–2017.

```{python}
#| label: setup-mo-runs
mo_fw = full_weekend_runs(production_performances(regs, minorcats=("music", "opera")))

mo_day_order = [d for d in WEEKDAY_ORDER if d in set(mo_fw["weekday"])]
mo_day = (
    mo_fw.groupby("weekday")
    .agg(performances=("swept_event_id", "count"), attendees=("attendees", "sum"))
    .reindex(mo_day_order)
)
mo_day["attendees per performance"] = mo_day["attendees"] / mo_day["performances"]
mo_day["share of attendees"] = mo_day["attendees"] / mo_day["attendees"].sum()
mo_rate = {d: f"{mo_day.loc[d, 'attendees per performance']:.0f}" for d in mo_day_order}
n_mo_runs = mo_fw["production_run"].nunique()
```

The `{python} f"{n_mo_runs}"` qualifying runs, chronologically:

```{python}
#| label: tab-mo-run-detail
mo_by_day = mo_fw.pivot_table(
    index="production_run",
    columns="weekday",
    values="attendees",
    aggfunc="sum",
    fill_value=0,
).reindex(columns=mo_day_order)
mo_by_day["Total"] = mo_by_day.sum(axis=1)
mo_meta = mo_fw.groupby("production_run").agg(
    first=("starts_on", "min"), last=("starts_on", "max"), performances=("swept_event_id", "count")
)
mo_order = mo_meta["first"].sort_values().index.tolist()
mo_detail = mo_meta.join(mo_by_day).reindex(mo_order)
mo_detail["first"] = mo_detail["first"].dt.strftime("%b %d, %Y")
mo_detail["last"] = mo_detail["last"].dt.strftime("%b %d, %Y")
mo_detail.index.name = None
mo_detail = mo_detail.rename(columns={"first": "First", "last": "Last", "performances": "Perfs"})
mo_detail.style.format(
    "{:,}", subset=[c for c in mo_detail.columns if c not in ("First", "Last")]
)
```

And by day of week:

```{python}
#| label: tab-mo-run-day
mo_day.style.format(
    {
        "performances": "{:,}",
        "attendees": "{:,}",
        "attendees per performance": "{:.1f}",
        "share of attendees": "{:.1%}",
    }
)
```

**Opera audiences didn't privilege the weekend: the midweek opera nights drew the most people
per performance (Thursday `{python} mo_rate.get('Thursday', '—')`, versus
`{python} mo_rate.get('Friday', '—')` on Friday and `{python} mo_rate.get('Saturday', '—')` on
Saturday, the weakest day per show — diluted, as in theater, by Saturday matinees).** The
sample is small (a few midweek performances, all in the 2013–2017 festival era), so treat the
midweek numbers as suggestive rather than precise.

## All events pooled, by day of week

The coarser view: every event of every kind, whether or not it was part of a run. Counts here
are **registrations** (a proxy for headcount), and because most events are scheduled on the day
the organizers chose, this mostly reflects *what gets booked* rather than audience preference —
use the within-run sections above for that. It is kept for context.

```{python}
#| label: tab-cat-weekday
cat_order = ["theater", "music", "dance", "opera", "class"]
named = regs[regs["event_minorcat"].isin(cat_order)].assign(
    cat=lambda d: d["event_minorcat"]
)
combo = pd.concat([named, regs[regs["event_majorcat"] == "class"].assign(cat="class")])
combo = combo.dropna(subset=["weekday"])
tab = combo.groupby(["cat", "weekday"], observed=True).size().reset_index(name="attendance")
pivot = (
    tab.pivot(index="cat", columns="weekday", values="attendance")
    .reindex(index=[c for c in cat_order if c in set(tab["cat"])])
    .reindex(columns=WEEKDAY_ORDER)
    .fillna(0)
    .astype(int)
)
pivot.style.format("{:,}")
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-weekday-facet
#| fig-cap: "Registrations by day of week — one panel per programming type"
fig = px.bar(
    tab,
    x="weekday",
    y="attendance",
    facet_col="cat",
    category_orders={"cat": cat_order, "weekday": WEEKDAY_ORDER},
    color_discrete_sequence=[BLUE],
)
fig.update_layout(yaxis_title="Registrations")
fig.for_each_xaxis(lambda a: a.update(title=""))
fig.update_traces(hovertemplate="%{y:,.0f}<extra></extra>")
style(fig).show()
```

**Theater peaks Saturday; music peaks Sunday. Classes spread across the week with a Friday
peak.**

Has the pooled day mix shifted? Same data as shares within each period (again: this moves when
the *schedule* moves, not necessarily the audience):

```{python}
#| label: fig-timing-over-time
#| fig-cap: "Registration share by day of week, by period (all events)"
pooled = regs.dropna(subset=["weekday"]).assign(
    period=lambda d: period(d["starts_on"].dt.year)
)
rd = pooled.groupby(["weekday", "period"], observed=True).size().reset_index(name="attendance")
rd["share"] = rd.groupby("period", observed=True)["attendance"].transform(
    lambda s: s / s.sum()
)
fig = px.line(
    rd, x="weekday", y="share", color="period",
    category_orders={"weekday": WEEKDAY_ORDER, "period": PERIODS},
    color_discrete_sequence=CATEGORICAL, markers=True,
)
fig.update_layout(
    xaxis_title="Day of week", yaxis_title="Share of registrations", legend_title="Period"
)
fig.update_yaxes(tickformat=".0%")
style(fig).show()
```

Finally, distance rather than volume: for **theater only**, registrations from each distance
band on each day of the week — "do Friday/Saturday theater audiences travel farther than
Sunday's?"

```{python}
#| label: tab-theater-day-band
theater = regs[regs["event_minorcat"] == "theater"].dropna(subset=["weekday", "distance_band"])
(
    theater.groupby(["weekday", "distance_band"], observed=True)
    .size()
    .unstack("distance_band")
    .reindex(WEEKDAY_ORDER)
    .fillna(0)
    .astype(int)
    .style.format("{:,}")
)
```
